profile.name: Klea Merkuri
profile.title: Software Engineer | Design Systems & Applied AI Solutions
profile.story: Software engineer in Los Angeles focused on design systems, cross-platform mobile (Flutter), and applied AI. Economics degree from UCLA (Summa Cum Laude). Currently at Finance of America; previously Experian. Builds tools including VersoID and writes about AI workflows on The Helpful Tipper.
profile.subtitle: I work on enterprise fintech platforms — and build the tools I wish already existed.
profile.lead: At Finance of America: design systems, property platforms, and Salesforce/Genesys integrations. Before that, a 5K-article Gatsby migration at Experian that cut load times ~20%.
about: I'm a software engineer in Los Angeles, currently building multi-tenant property platforms, design systems, and AI integrations for enterprise fintech.

I didn't come up through a CS program. I studied Economics at UCLA (Summa Cum Laude) and got hooked on the part of the work that felt like engineering: tracing how data moves, where it breaks, and what has to change for systems to stay in sync.

At Finance of America, I own lead capture flows wired into Salesforce and Genesys and the design system keeping those properties WCAG-compliant. Before that, at Experian, I led a headless Gatsby migration across 5,000+ blog articles — cut load times ~20% — and built Node APIs that moved BI data from quarterly manual pulls to real-time sync.

Outside work, I ship tools I actually use: VersoID, a Flutter app for managing professional personas via QR codes (live on iOS), plus Chrome extensions and MCP experiments documented on The Helpful Tipper.
